name: Wonder of the Seas
description: Wonder of the Seas is the world's largest cruise ship by gross tonnage. It features eight distinct neighborhoods, a zip line, 10 water slides, an ice skating rink, a surf simulator, and a wide variety of dining and entertainment options.

recreation: 4 pools including H2O Zone kids waterpark
included: All meals in Main Dining Room and Windjammer buffet
Live entertainment and Broadway shows
Kids & teens clubs (Adventure Ocean)
Access to pools, fitness center, and sports areas
Flowrider surf simulator and rock climbing wall

name: Icon of the Seas
description: Icon of the Seas debuted in 2024 as the world's largest cruise ship. It features six water slides, the largest waterpark at sea, eight neighborhoods, and a tropical garden, representing the next generation of Royal Caribbean mega-ships.

entertainment: Category 6 waterpark (6 slides — largest at sea)
RipCord by iFly indoor skydiving simulator
recreation: 6 pools including Chill Island and Surfside family area
included: All meals in Main Dining Room and Windjammer buffet
Live entertainment and shows
Kids & teens clubs (Adventure Ocean)
Access to all pools, fitness center, and sports areas
Flowrider surf simulator and rock climbing wall

name: Symphony of the Seas
description: Symphony of the Seas was the world's largest cruise ship when it launched in 2018. A sister ship to Wonder of the Seas, it features the same eight neighborhoods including Central Park, Boardwalk, and the thrilling Perfect Storm waterslides.

recreation: 4 pools including H2O Zone kids waterpark
included: All meals in Main Dining Room and Windjammer buffet

name: Harmony of the Seas

name: Oasis of the Seas
description: Oasis of the Seas pioneered a new era of cruising when it debuted in 2009 as the first Oasis-class ship. It introduced the revolutionary neighborhood concept — seven distinct areas including a Central Park, Boardwalk, and Royal Promenade — that transformed modern cruise ship design.

recreation: 4 pools including H2O Zone kids waterpark

name: Quantum of the Seas
description: Quantum of the Seas introduced a wave of innovations when it launched in 2014, including the North Star observation pod that lifts guests 300 feet above sea level, RipCord by iFly skydiving simulator, and Two70 — an immersive entertainment venue with 270-degree views.

dining: Main Dining Room (American Icon, Chic, Silk, The Grande)
entertainment: RipCord by iFly indoor skydiving simulator
SeaPlex indoor sports complex (bumper cars, roller skating, circus school)
included: All meals in main dining venues and Windjammer buffet
Live entertainment and shows
Kids & teens clubs (Adventure Ocean)
Access to pools and fitness center
Complimentary North Star rides and iFly sessions (limited per sailing)

name: Anthem of the Seas
description: Anthem of the Seas is the second Quantum-class ship and the largest ship based year-round in the UK. It shares its sister ship's signature innovations — North Star, iFly, Two70 — and is known for its Wonderland surrealist restaurant and SeaPlex, the largest indoor activity complex at sea.
